_README: Single source of truth for NOC alert scenarios (see docs/plans/NOC_SCENARIO_CATALOG.md). Each entry drives: the live alert seeder (alert.* -> /api/3/alerts), the Fabric Studio fault recipe (induce.*), the BASELINE precondition config (baseline.* -> a non-destructive FS recipe so the fault is REAL against live FAZ/FMG), and the offline sim fixtures (fmg_row + faz_logs). `target.*` holds the per-site FGT facts (device/mgmt_ip/jump_host) and is PLACEHOLDER (${...}) until the real Fabric Studio devices are known — fill these in before the live drive. `device` ties the agent's tool calls to the right story. The FS scenarios.yaml recipes are GENERATED from this manifest via python/gen_fs_recipes.py. device_down lives in code in _sim_fixtures.py for now (FGT-BRANCH-04) and is documented here for the seeder/FS side only.

alert.title: Site-to-site VPN HQ↔BRANCH07 is down — branch isolated
alert.description: FortiAnalyzer reported repeated IPsec phase2-down events for tunnel HQ-BRANCH07 on FGT-BRANCH-07 starting 03:22. The branch LAN (10.70.0.0/16) can no longer reach HQ resources. The device itself is still reporting to FortiManager (reachable), so this is a tunnel/VPN failure, not a device outage.

target._comment: PLACEHOLDER. Two managed FGTs for the VPN slice. Fill device/mgmt_ip/jump_host from Fabric Studio before the live drive; the generator substitutes these into the FS recipe scope/connection.

baseline.comment: Preconditions so the VPN fault is REAL against live FAZ/FMG: a working HQ<->branch IPsec phase1/phase2 tunnel, FAZ logging enabled, and FMG central-management registration. NON-DESTRUCTIVE; run once at lab bring-up on BOTH FGTs. peer/local addrs and subnets differ per side, so the generator emits one baseline recipe per side (setup_hq / setup_branch). Placeholders (${...}) come from target.*.

probe.command: diagnose vpn tunnel list name HQ-BRANCH07
probe.comment: Run on the branch FGT after both sides are up; expect a phase2 SA with 'status=up' and installed selectors.

baseline.assert_up: tunnel HQ-BRANCH07 shows an established phase2 SA (status=up); FAZ device list shows ${FGT_BRANCH} logging; FMG fleet shows ${FGT_BRANCH} registered + insync.
baseline.verify_after: Once green, faz_search_device_events(devid=${FGT_BRANCH}, logtype="vpn") returns REAL phase2 logs and fmg_get_device_status shows the device up — NOT the sim fixture.

induce.comment: Disable the IPsec phase1-interface on the branch FGT; the tunnel drops and FAZ logs phase2-down. teardown re-enables it. REQUIRES the baseline above already applied.

faz_logs.msg: IPsec phase2 SA HQ-BRANCH07 negotiation failed (retry 4)
IPsec phase1 HQ-BRANCH07 disabled by admin / down
Device healthy; WAN1 up, FortiManager keepalive OK
